texts.full_text: you to answer to the various aggrieved parties for that. But this isn't necessary, because I understand that it's unfair to tar an immense and complex religio-cultural legacy for things that are best understood in the context of regional history, theocratic imperialism, social and political evolution, and a long, long list of other relevant phenomena. I do not, though, consider facile rationalizations to the effect of "Well they weren't 'true Christians' " or "the Christian ideal has been found difficult and left untried" to be adequate explanations for all these things, and I wouldn't expect anyone to consider the Muslim equivalent of such rationalizations adequate either. However, when you treat scriptural support (or lack of it) as sufficient case for passing such blanket judgments, you are backing your opponents into a corner and pretty much guaranteeing that unworkable conclusions like "Christians are inevitably unreliable hypocrites" or "Muslims are inevitably called to slaughter" will result. It's a worthlessly defeatist strategy. If you are serious about "knowing your enemy," and have a constructive end in mind for doing so, you will not rely on such arguments. Yes, theologically derived ideologies (scriptural or not) must be contended with, but so must historical, political, economic, demographic and cultural factors. Hamilton Notable awards Royal Medal (1938) Guy Medal in Gold (1946) Copley Medal (1955) Sir Ronald Aylmer Fisher FRS (17 February 1890 – 29 July 1962) was an English statistician, evolutionary biologist, and geneticist. He is often referred to as one of the founders of modern statistical science and contributed significantly to applied statistics through his work on experimental design methods such as analysis of variance (ANOVA). Fisher's contributions also extended into genetics where he developed fundamental theories about natural selection alongside JBS Haldane, Sewall Wright. Fisher was born in East Finchley London England the son George and Katie Heath his twin brother died shortly after birth only two of their seven children survived infancy Fisher's mother dying when she 49 years old while undergoing surgery intended improve her eyesight which had deteriorated due diabetes His father became bankrupt later life leaving young Ronald support himself financially through scholarships tutoring During schooling Winchester College excelled mathematics winning Neeld Medal his final year He studied at Gonville and Caius college Cambridge from 1908 initially focusing solely on maths but switching major part way towards physics due influence mentor James Jeans who encouraged interest astronomy Upon graduating worked City London firm mercantile marine insurance however left join military World War I After war ended took up post statistician Rothamsted agricultural research station Hertfords

texts.full_text: uced proconvulsant activity of caffeine in rats after a series of electroconvulsive seizures. A variety of neurotransmitter receptor changes occur after a course of electroconvulsive seizures (ECS) in rats, including an increased density of adenosine A1 sites. Adenosine antagonism has been related to the proconvulsant action of methylxanthines such as caffeine. We determined tonic-clonic seizure threshold and duration evoked by subcutaneous administration (0, 25 or 78 mg/kg) of various doses in rats that had received six ECS treatments over the course of three weeks with two shocks per day, as well as control animals who did not receive any shock therapy. We also determined threshold and duration for caffeine-induced seizures prior to initiating electroconvulsive treatment (ECS). The present study demonstrates that increasing doses significantly increased both the tonic phase latency time taken from injection until onset of rigidity or convulsion, and maximum clonic episodes' temporal extent per rat after series completion compared before; however while these measures were moderately prolonged at 25mg/kg dose post-ECS relative pre-, they continued increasing under higher doses showing greater elevation above baseline values following electroshocks ending. This will allow URLs to contain both Latin and Arabic characters. Note, however, that domain names typically do not include non-ASCII characters directly; they use Punycode encoding for internationalized domain names (IDNs). For paths or query parameters in the URL, including Arabic letters can be more relevant. texts.full_text: process three more times to complete the full four-lap course of drinking and sprinting - all in an effort to claim bragging rights as Austin Beer Mile champion; sounds simple enough but trust me when I say it is anything but. The combination of lactate buildup from intense running mixed with a bellyful of rapidly consumed beer makes for some rather... interesting outcomes, ranging anywhere between impressive feats of athleticism and cringe-worthy displays of inebriated staggering - sometimes within the span of just one lap! As participants gathered at the starting line on that fateful day back in 2011, an air of uncertainty hung over them like a thick fog: who would reign supreme? Would it be seasoned athletes leveraging their honed skills to outpace and out-chug all comers or perhaps some dark horse candidate rising up from relative obscurity with uncanny beer-handling abilities that defied logic itself. Only time, beers downed in quick succession coupled by the ensuing mad dashes around this makeshift track would ultimately tell us who'd claim top honors

ample with recursive comments and replies using `ng-model`, you're encountering the default behavior where both controllers share access due to their prototypical relationship. This isn't inherently "wrong" but might not be what's desired for certain use cases, such as when each comment or reply should maintain its own independent state. ### How Can I Avoid Shared Scopes? To ensure that `ng-model` in the child controller does not affect the parent scope (or vice versa), you can create an isolated scope. This is often done using a directive with specific configuration for isolation, but since your use case involves controllers rather than directives directly managing scopes, here are some approaches: 1. Use Directive Isolation: Create custom components or directives that encapsulate their own behavior and isolate the model. ```javascript app.directive('commentBox', function() { return { restrict: 'E', scope: {}, // This creates an isolated child scope. template: '' + '' + '' }; }); app.controller('ParentController', function($scope, commentService){ // Logic to handle sending comments $scope.$on("sendComment", function(event, data){ console.log(data); sendNewData(); }); ... })
// Recursive comment
``` 2. Use `ng-model-options` with a Specific Binding Context: You can pass down the model to children in such a way that it uses its own context, like using an object structure. ```html
...
``` 3. Pass Model as a Parameter: Pass specific data to each child controller by using the `=` binding, effectively creating unique copies for each instance. ```html
Someone said: {{parentComment}} ...
``` ### Conclusion Using isolated scopes or passing specific model data ensures that changes in one part of your application do not inadvertently affect another. The choice between these methods depends on the complexity and requirements (such as reusability) for each component within your AngularJS app. By using directives with their own scope, you can encapsulate functionality more effectively while avoiding unwanted side effects from shared models

Players control mostly the military action of their nation. This includes such activities as drafting forces, building forts and depots, sieges and blockades, raIding enemy settlements and battles, both on land and at sea. Birth of America has two wars, the French and Indian War and the American Revolution. The object of the game is to attack and capture objectives, strategic towns, and manage strategies in order to win battles thus gaining edge. Real political events, such as the battle of Bunker Hill and the signing of the Declaration of Independence, will also influence the outcome of morale throughout the game. texts.full_text: it was observed that the transfecting efficiency of plasmid DNA complexed with Pronectin F(+), regardless of the kind of cationized Pronectin F(+), was higher than that of plasmid DNA complexed with the gelatin derivative. These results indicate that the cationized derivatives of Pronectin F(+) with RGD sequences are potentially effective non-viral gene carriers. To summarize, this study explored the use of cationized derivatives of Pronectin F(+) as a non-viral gene carrier in the context of gene transfection. By introducing ethylenediamine, spermidine, and spermine to Pronectin F(+), researchers aimed to enhance its efficacy as a gene carrier. Comparing these derivatives to their gelatin counterparts, which contain one RGD sequence per molecule, the study found that Pronectin F(+) derivatives provided superior transfecting efficiency in RGM-1 cells. This suggests that Pronectin F(+) and its cationized derivatives could be promising candidates for further research and development in non-viral gene delivery systems. The study underscores the importance of RGD sequences in mediating cellular uptake

texts.full_text: 975, the Steelers won back-to-back Super Bowls. In 1974, Thomas picked up a five interceptions and he scored a touchdown on a fumble recovery. Thomas was named to the Pro Bowl after a 1976 season in which he collected two interceptions and a fumble recovery. In 1978, he sat out the season due to a blood disorder known as Boeck's sarcoid. During his time in Pittsburgh, Thomas played alongside cornerback Mel Blount. NFL.com named Thomas and Blount the cornerback combination of all time. During the 1982 preseason, Thomas was traded to the Denver Broncos for an initially draft slot. Thomas has lived in Monroeville, Pennsylvania, since the 1970s. Partnering with former Steeler Larry Brown in the late 1980s, Thomas opened an Applebee's restaurant in the Monroeville area. In 2009, he opened a restaurant known as Red, Hot & Blue in Homestead, PEnnsylvania. References External links Florida State Seminoles bio J.T. texts.full_text: High glucose levels in diabetic patients are known to enhance WNT/β-catenin signaling—a pathway critical for cell fate determination and embryonic development. Aberrant activation of this signaling has been implicated in various cancers, suggesting a possible mechanism linking hyperglycemia-driven changes to cancer risk elevation seen with diabetes. The enhanced WNT/β-catenin activity due to high glucose might contribute by promoting the transcriptional upregulation of oncogenes and downregulating tumor suppressor genes. This dysregulated pathway could lead to increased cell proliferation, resistance to apoptosis (programmed cell death), and ultimately facilitating a tumorigenic environment. Additionally, hyperglycemia-induced oxidative stress may also play an important role in this process by causing DNA damage or modifying signaling pathways that further promote cancer development. Understanding these mechanisms more deeply can provide insights into potential therapeutic targets for reducing the risk of diabetes-associated cancers through modulation of glucose levels and WNT/β-catenin pathway activity. Further research is needed to elucidate all molecular players involved, how they interact in diabetic conditions specifically contributing to oncogenesis, as well as identifying biomarkers that could help predict cancer susceptibility among diabetics. texts.full_text: all-out war footing due to their early successes in the West. Hitler still thought victory would be quick and decisive in the East and did not see the need to fully mobilize the German industrial capability. Also of great interest is the widely differing approaches to weapons design and manufacturing. The German armaments companies wanted the best designed and built weapons systems. Adhering to that strategy always takes longer and costs more. Even when the German military asked for weapons that could be brought to the field more quickly with a little less quality, the historical German approach to weapons design and manufacture was slow to change. The Russians deployed a quantity-versus-quality war-making mentality that tHey endorsed until the end of the Cold War. Zitadelle is not for the novice WWII buff who wants a light overview about this critical key battle. The level of depth and detail could soon overwhelm the casual reader looking for a quick snapshot of this point in history. It is, however, a great book for the WWII aficionado who wants to delve into the details from both a military and economic perspective that brought about this huge clash of armored forces. Interpretive Summary: Technical Abstract: The objectives of this study were to evaluate the effects of planting date on seedling emergence of two varieties of switchgrass. An experiment was conducted at the University of Missouri South Farm in Columbia, MO (38°55’N, 92°12’W) during autumn 2005 and spring 2006. The experimental design was a randomized complete block with three replications. Main plots consisted of planting dates (October 7 or November 4 for fall seeding; march 23 or April 19 for spring seeding). The bThe development of limited-sampling strategies for estimating mycophenolic acid's AUC(0-12h) in renal transplant recipients taking enteric-coated Myfortric® is crucial due to the lack thereof. Here’s an outline based on your provided information: Objective: To develop and validate a Limited-Sample Strategy (LSS), using multiple regression analysis, for estimating mycophenolic acid AUC(0-12h) in patients receiving tacrolimus with enteric-coated Myfortric®. ### Methodology #### Study Design The study included 71 renal transplant recipients treated concurrently and long-term on the combination of EC-MPS (Myfortric®, MedsBiotech Co., Ltd.) + Tac. Serial blood samples were collected over a period covering up to two hours post-dose at times: pre, immediate-post dose administration (+5 min), then hourly from 1 hour through twelve. #### Analytical Method Plasma concentrations of mycophenolic acid (MPA) and tacrolimus levels in plasma or serum was determined using an enzyme-multiplied immunoassay technique. The pharmacokinetic parameters were calculated with non-compartmental methods, employing WinNonlin software version 8.x. #### Statistical Analysis for LSS Development - Multiple Regression Models: A series of regression models (full model and reduced one) containing different combinations from all possible time points to the full set was used. Test Group vs Validation Set: The dataset comprising MPA concentrations in plasma were divided into two groups: 1. Test group ( = 47): Used for building multiple linear regressions models based on various LSS candidates selected by Akaike Information Criteria (AIC). 2. External validation set n= 25 : The remaining dataset was utilized to validate the final model chosen. ### Results - Identification of Optimal Sampling Times: From initial screening, five different sampling strategies were shortlisted as potential models for estimating AUC(0-inf). - For example: Model1 = pre-dose and post-treatment (t+5min) These candidate LSSs then underwent further analysis using the test dataset. - Validation Process of Selected Models: Once identified, each selected model was rigorously validated on an independent external validation set. ### Conclusion The study aimed to establish

State v. Di Giosia , 3 N.J. 413 (1950); State v. Labato , 7 N.J. 137 (1951). In the case before us, the first indictment on which sentence was suspended, charged a robbery of which "A" was the victim, and added the allegation of prior convictions. If the particular robbery had not been proved, the jury would have had to acquit, even though the prior convictions had been admitted. The indictment on which appellant was sentenced to imprisonment for life, charged a robbery upon "B." Proof of that robbery would have been immaterial on the trial of the first indictment and proof of robbing "A" would not have been admissible on the trial of the indictment for robbing "B." texts.full_text: at River Bend Winery & Vineyard FLIP PHOTOS THUMBNAILS Luc Anthony and Dave Benning with grand prize winner Mandy Jones. A group of young people from the local community gathered to support The ARC Foundation on Saturday, August 1st for a wine tasting party. The event took place in a new winery at River Bend Vineyard located near Chatham. Tickets were sold prior to the event and all proceeds were donated to The ARC Foundation to benefit children with Down Syndrome. The evening started out as a beautiful summer day. Although it did rain, there was no dampening of spirits! It was so much fun to see young people coming together for an important cause. Everyone enjoyed the wine tasting and then purchased bottles or cases of their favorite wines from River Bend Winery. We had a lot of great door prizes donated by local businesses which made the evening more fun. The grand prize, an overnight stay at the President Abraham Lincoln Hotel & Conference Center in Springfield was awarded to Mandy Jones who is a volunteer with The ARC Fou

texts.full_text: e text, based on the information provided: "We explore five properties that contribute to the high rate of credit card borrowing among US households. First, the calibrated labor income path slopes upward early in life, indicating that households have more earning potential earlier in their working years. This can lead to fluctuations in household finances due to transitory income shocks. Second, consumers invest actively in an illiquid asset, such as a home, which cannot be easily sold or liquidated to smooth transitory income shocks. Third, the presence of dependents, such as children, can increase financial responsibilities and stress, leading to a higher demand for credit card borrowing. Fourth, consumers may declare bankruptcy, reducing the effective cost of credit card borrowing. Finally, we find that hyperbolic time preferences can help explain the observed high rate of credit card borrowing, as simulated hyperbolic consumers borrow actively in the revolving credit card market and accumulate relatively large stocks of illiquid wealth." Our calibrated model predicts that 20% of the population will borrow on their credit card at any point in time, far less than the observed rate of over 60%. We identify a resolution to this puzzle: hyperbolic time preferences. Simulated hyperbolic consumers borrow actively in the revolving credit card market and accumulate relatively large stocks of illiquid wealth, matching observed data. (2780 K) T

texts.full_text: concerned with the biosynthesis of oncornavirus proteins and how translational and post-translational events act to control viral multiplication. We are currently investigating several aspects of viral protein biosynthesis: (1) Viral gene expression in normal uninfected tissues: We are involved in the identification and characterization of a series of viral-related polyproteins synthesized by normal chicken embryo tissues. Some of these proteins may be tissue-specific, while others appear to be ubiquitous; their role in the physiology or pathology of virus infection is not yet clear. (2) Mechanisms controlling translation: We are studying how oncornaviruses regulate protein synthesis during different stages of viral replication and maturation by examining changes in polysome structure, messenger RNA activity, and initiation factor function that occur as a result of viral gene expression; these studies have led to the identification of novel translational control mechanisms. texts.full_text: d Russian firms. Non-performing loans were rising even before interest rates were raised to 9.5% to defend the rouble. Meanwhile Russian banks are reliant on the central bank to replace deposits that their customers are understandably spiriting into dollars. Directly or indirectly, many of these bills will end up with the Kremlin, which is why its reserves will be vital. They are evaporating: down $100 billion in the past year, following failed attempts to defend the rouble. And the book-keeping is dodgy. Of the reported $370 billion reserve pile, more than $170 billion sits in the country’s two wealth funds. Some of their assets are iffy, including various stakes in Russia’s state-owned banks and debt issued by Ukraine that Mr Putin’s own aggression is fast rendering worthless. One of the funds is earmarked for pensions. In reality, Russia’s government has perhaps $270 billion of hard cash that is accessible and usable without massive cuts elsewhere—less than its external obligations due over the next two years. All this spells trouble for Russia, but Mr Putin’s marauding foreign policy could accelerate things. This after all is a man who has invaded other countries and lied about it. A deeper foray into Ukraine would lead to stronger sanctions by Western countries. Some of them, such as barring Russia’s banks from the SWIFT international payments system, could halt Russian trade altogether. A partial block on oil exports would fell the economy, as it did Iran’s. And the more trouble he faces, the more likely Mr Putin is to play the nationalist card—and that means more foreign forays, and yet more sanctions. This time a string of bank failures, corporate defaults and a deep recession look likelier.
